phase.name: Phase 1: bin/griot CLI + obvious wrapper kills

unit: D1: Scaffold bin/griot + port use verb

contract.goal: Scaffold the bin/griot CLI entry with a flat-verb dispatcher (mirroring bin/draft's shape), port .claude/scripts/griot/use.ts to a verb-handler under the new structure, and ensure bin/griot use is byte-for-byte equivalent to node .claude/scripts/griot/use.ts. First of four deliverables in Phase 1 (D1 use+scaffold, D2 operator-checks+mediate-panel, D3 capture, D4 wrapper kills + permissions cleanup). All four ship in one PR.
acceptance_criteria: bin/griot shim exists, modeled on bin/draft/bin/loom (bash execs node .claude/cli/griot.ts).
.claude/cli/griot.ts is a flat-verb dispatcher modeled on .claude/cli/draft.ts; imports GRIOT_VERBS; registers the use verb with help text.
.claude/cli/verbs/griot/use.ts exports useVerb(rest, ctx) returning DispatchResult; output is byte-equivalent to the existing script when given the same cwd.
.claude/cli/verbs/griot/index.ts exports GRIOT_VERBS = { use: useVerb } plus GriotCliContext / DispatchResult / GriotVerbHandler types.
Sibling tests at .claude/cli/verbs/griot/use.test.ts and .claude/cli/griot.test.ts cover parity scenarios + dispatcher behavior + a spawnSync smoke test against bin/griot. All pass.
npm run lint clean; npm test clean.
.claude/scripts/griot/use.ts is preserved (deletion is D4's scope); both paths work during the migration.
rules_applied: Mirror bin/draft's flat-verb architecture (per existing Decision rationale in projects/2026-05-15-draft-cli).
Erasable TypeScript only; no enums, no namespaces (L-001 + types.ts convention).
L-001: invoke TypeScript via node directly; no tsx.
disqualifiers: Delete or modify .claude/scripts/griot/use.ts or its sibling test (D4's scope).
Modify the output format of use (status line text, citation contract phrases, antipattern curation).
Diverge from bin/draft's flat-verb dispatcher architecture.
Hand-edit PLAN.md or substrate-managed JSON outside of bin/loom or bin/draft verbs.

actions: Pre-flight: switched to main, fast-forwarded origin/main (b923b0c), cut new branch substrate-cli/phase-1.
bin/loom phase update substrate-cli 1 --status=in-progress --branch=substrate-cli/phase-1 (emits phase-started event).
Wrote bin/griot shim (18 lines, exec node .claude/cli/griot.ts), chmod +x.
Wrote .claude/cli/griot.ts dispatcher (131 lines, flat-verb shape patterned on draft.ts: parseInvocation, formatHelp, formatUnknownVerbError, dispatch, main, isEntryPoint).
Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/griot/use.ts (ported logic from .claude/scripts/griot/use.ts; returns DispatchResult, accepts cwd via GriotCliContext for testability).
Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/griot/use.test.ts (14 tests adapted from use.test.ts to call useVerb directly with a tmpdir cwd).
Wrote .claude/cli/griot.test.ts (17 tests: parseInvocation/formatHelp/formatUnknownVerbError unit tests + dispatch tests + spawnSync smoke tests against bin/griot).
Smoke-tested bin/griot --help, bin/griot bogus, bin/griot use; all behaviors match expected.
Confirmed byte-for-byte parity: diff <(bin/griot use) <(node .claude/scripts/griot/use.ts) exit 0 against the live repo's learnings/rollup.md.
Ran npm run lint (clean, 129 files, 2 pre-existing broken-symlink warnings unrelated to this diff).
Ran npm test (35 files / 461 tests pass; +2 files +31 tests from this unit).

Skipped the whiteboard step for this phase: 5 of 6 registered whiteboard-* engineers (a11y, design-systems, performance, react-architect, sketch-ideation) are aimed at aart.camp's UI/sketch surface, not substrate-CLI plumbing; the whiteboard-substrate-engineer that would be most relevant is being authored in this project's Phase 5. Documented in notes for the PR.

Composed dense evaluator packet; invoked /guild-validate with agents=evaluator-contract-fit,evaluator-naming. Both evaluators returned VERDICT: approved with no blocking or advisory findings; aggregated verdict is approved.

scope: Port the use verb (byte-equivalent to the legacy script).
Sibling vitest tests for both the verb and the dispatcher.
Phase 1 row in manifest moved to in-progress on branch substrate-cli/phase-1.
changes_since_previous: First checkin on this branch; baseline is origin/main at b923b0c (post PR #97 merge of PLAN.md revision).
notes_for_pr: D1 of 4 in Phase 1. PR will collect D1+D2+D3+D4 before request-for-review.
Byte-for-byte parity verified: diff <(bin/griot use) <(node .claude/scripts/griot/use.ts) exit 0 against the live rollup.md.

Manual override on panel composition: derive-panel.ts could not parse PANEL-COMPOSITION.md and fell back to FALLBACK_RULES (stderr: 'derive-panel-error: panel-spec-unreadable (using fallback)' + 'mapping-section-missing'). Fallback rules don't include the `.claude/cli/**/*.ts` carve-out from the spec, so the script's stdout incorrectly included evaluator-react-api. Per PANEL-COMPOSITION.md the correct panel for .claude/cli/**/*.ts is contract-fit + naming; ran with that manually. Filing this as a substrate finding for a sibling project to address; not in D1's scope.

Whiteboard step skipped this phase: 5 of 6 registered whiteboard-* engineers (a11y, design-systems, performance, react-architect, sketch-ideation) are aimed at aart.camp's UI/sketch surface; the substrate-engineer engineer that would be most relevant is being authored in this project's Phase 5. Documented as an intentional carve-out, not a substrate violation.

Manifest-vs-PLAN drift: manifest.json's phases[] still has only the placeholder Phase 1 from the scaffold (#96); PLAN.md (post #97) has 6 phases. bin/loom phase update worked for Phase 1, but Phases 2-6 will need a manifest-write verb that doesn't yet exist. This is the deferred #4 substrate gap (sibling project); doesn't block D1-D4 but will block later phases of this same project.
Legacy script .claude/scripts/griot/use.ts preserved — both paths work; D4 will delete the legacy.
